description: Cloth produced by weaving or knitting; material with texture and drape. 'Liru' names the flowing textile—threads interlaced into continuous surface.
sound_symbolism: The word flows in threads. 'Li' moves with liquid continuity, fibers streaming. Then 'ru' rolls onward, fabric extending endlessly.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo liru' (the fabric). Emphasizes texture and flow more than 'wetha' (cloth). Used for quality textiles.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fabric as art—the weaver's skill creating texture, pattern, beauty in daily material.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Flowing fabric—drape and movement, textile as living form.
